behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: CSV row parsing (row number), schema validation, and DLQ writer must preserve metadata together. A unit test of only the validator would miss mapping bugs (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no AI output, no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher level: behavior text only says rows are rejected and written to DLQ with row number; it does not require real S3/Postgres/network/process restart.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + validator + DLQ adapter in-process. Feed CSV rows with missing required fields at known line numbers. Assert invalid rows are sent to DLQ entries containing exact original row numbers and valid rows are excluded from DLQ.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is pure transformation logic on input strings; minimum real failure boundary is one function/object with no I/O. MOCK-MISS: failure is parsing/normalization logic itself, not module wiring. Rejected higher level using behavior text: it only specifies converting date formats, with no DB/S3/network/process dependency and no AI judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Test the date-normalization function directly with ISO 8601, US, and European samples (including ambiguous dates). Assert canonical output and explicit failure behavior for invalid/ambiguous inputs.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real runtime memory/timeout behavior (REAL-INFRA): OS memory pressure, allocator behavior, and wall-clock execution under large load. In-process mocks can hide OOM/timeout failures (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected lower levels: unit/integration cannot reliably surface real memory exhaustion or timeout dynamics at this scale.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline as a real process on a 500k-row fixture with production-like configuration. Measure completion time against configured timeout and monitor peak RSS/heap to ensure no OOM. Verify process exit status and batch completion metrics.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets real external API error handling semantics (S3 503) plus retry/backoff timing; this is a System boundary (real HTTP/subprocess timing behavior). A pure mock may always pass while missing SDK/HTTP integration quirks (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected higher levels: text is deterministic retry behavior, not AI/LLM-dependent and not multi-agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute pipeline against an HTTP/S3-compatible endpoint that returns one or more 503 responses before success. Assert retry attempts occur with increasing delays matching backoff policy and that batch processing continues after recovery.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes process restart plus durable DB state/transaction semantics. Real duplication bugs often appear only across true restart and real PostgreSQL behavior (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Integration fakes may miss idempotency and commit-boundary issues. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM output quality dependency and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against a real PostgreSQL instance, intentionally terminate/restart mid-batch, then resume processing. Assert final table has exactly one record per source row key (no duplicates) and counts match expected unique rows.
